title: armeria pubescens subsp. expansa
intro: Armeria pubescens subsp. expansa, also known as the marsh thistle or sticky thistle, is a plant species belonging to the family Plantaginaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Armeria pubescens subsp. expansa include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health ailments.
